pediatricHealthContext.plainEnglishMeaning: Pediatric Health is represented as an evidence-boundary package. It can summarize supplied local evidence and references, while marking unsupported personal conclusions unavailable.
pediatricHealthContext.professionalReview: Use qualified professional review for pediatric, developmental, school, screening, medication, and family decisions; this synthetic local result is not enough for those decisions.

professionalReview: Use this local result as educational context for pediatric health genetics boundary context, not as a decision tool.
Collect validated source output, source provenance, and relevant clinical or professional context before using Pediatric Health for personal decisions.
Discuss pediatric, developmental, school, screening, medication, and family decisions with qualified professionals when those choices matter.

Report unavailable pediatric health genetics context evidence explicitly; do not diagnose pediatric conditions, predict child development, growth, behavior, learning, safety risk, disease risk, medication response, supplement need, diet need, school need, therapy need, vaccine action, testing need, screening need, treatment need, parenting action, or clinical actionability from genotype.
uncertainty: No validated pediatric health genetics boundary context model with source provenance and professional review is supplied for this local package.
The supplied rows support evidence-boundary education only and should not be treated as a personal prediction or clinical interpretation.
limitations: This validation result uses selected derived evidence only; raw genome data, VCF, FASTQ, BAM, CRAM, and complete callset files are not included.
The package does not recreate unavailable Sequencing.com authenticated report rows or proprietary third-party output for Pediatric Health.
The package does not support: child diagnosis; developmental prognosis; pediatric screening recommendation; school or activity restriction; medication advice; family-member risk conclusion.
